purpose: Preserve the ordered relationship between queue intent, implementation commits, evidence commits, acceptance boundaries, unresolved external outcomes, and later supersession without treating commit order as causal proof.

included: abstract project decisions and evidence relations

chronology_rules.temporal_order: Commit timestamps and queue snapshots establish ordering only.
chronology_rules.causality: A later commit may implement, document, test, or supersede an earlier decision, but chronology alone is not causal proof.
chronology_rules.status: Current-status propositions may supersede earlier status while preserving the earlier observation as historically valid.
chronology_rules.evidence_layers: Queue intent, source implementation, verifier execution, deployment, runtime outcome, and peer execution require separate evidence.

records.subject: continuity_mining/M-001

decision.proposition: Build a provenance-preserving continuity index while excluding private material.

evidence.proposition: Five records were retained with claim state, evidence strength, source identity, privacy class, and review route.

current_status.proposition: The earlier active status was superseded by completed; the earlier snapshot remains historically valid.

records.next_relation: The handoff proposed adding commit and decision-history records and linking contradictions instead of overwriting observations.

records.subject: continuity_mining/M-002

decision.proposition: Convert the M-001 handoff into an explicit status-transition ledger.
decision.basis: M-001 handoff plus later continuity commit sequence

evidence.proposition: Three transitions distinguish supersession from coexistence and retain review routes.

unresolved: Vercel iPhone audible outcome remains unverified.

records.subject: continuity_mining/M-003

decision.proposition: Normalize project evidence language so implementation, verification, deployment, runtime outcome, and triggering cannot be collapsed into one success claim.
decision.basis: Repeated cross-team ambiguity visible in queue and evidence summaries

evidence.proposition: Five terms now have scoped meanings, decision boundaries, provenance, and review routes.

unresolved: Whether current provider deployments match latest relevant commits.
Whether iPhone Safari produced audible output for the deployed audio slice.

decision.proposition: Resolve and prove the actual Cloudflare Pages hostname and deployment.

evidence_boundary.proposition: The workflow and acceptance artifacts exist, but existence does not prove a resolving deployment or cryptographically verified signature.

missing: served identity bound to source commit

decision.proposition: Individual verifier receipts require a complete policy-level summary before delegated evidence acceptance.
decision.basis: R-007 receipt contract followed by R-008 verification summary attestation

evidence_boundary.proposition: Unsigned summary attestations organize verification results but do not establish cryptographic authenticity, signer trust, deployment truth, or underlying claim truth.

next_action.proposition: Cloudflare Research should generate complete receipts, build and sign the summary, then verify signer and verifier identity before acceptance.

resolved.apparent_conflict: A queue item is completed while later work continues in the same domain.
resolved.resolution: Completion applies to the scoped item; later items extend the substrate and do not invalidate prior completion.

resolved.apparent_conflict: Evidence commits follow implementation commits and appear to prove execution.
resolved.resolution: They prove durable evidence records were committed; verifier execution, deployment, and runtime outcomes still require their own retained receipts.

resolved.apparent_conflict: The aggregate queue is older than additive queue-update commits.
resolved.resolution: The aggregate queue is a stale snapshot; owner-scoped additive updates are later observations and must be reconciled rather than silently overwriting the snapshot.

unresolved: No canonical reconciler currently computes current queue state from the aggregate snapshot plus additive owner updates.
External deployment and iPhone audibility outcomes remain unproven.

verification.method: JSON structural review, source SHA cross-check, and recent commit-order comparison
verification.acceptance: Each chronology record separates decision, implementation, evidence, unresolved outcome, and next action while preserving source identity and claim state.
